opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Water got in my ear in the shower, and suddenly it went completely deaf and started throbbing intensely.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly this evening right after a shower — my hearing's gone in that ear, there's loud ringing, and it really hurts, and I'm scared it might be permanent.

symptom_narrative.onset: About three weeks of mild fullness and occasional itch in the ear, then this evening after a shower it suddenly blocked completely.
symptom_narrative.duration: The sudden blockage, ringing and pain started this evening and haven't let up.
symptom_narrative.character: A totally blocked, plugged-up feeling in the ear, a loud high-pitched ringing, and a throbbing ache.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: The pain is moderate to severe and the deafness on that side is really distressing.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: It came on in steps right after the shower and has stayed constant since.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: It came on straight after water got into the ear in the shower.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Loud ringing in the ear (tinnitus), a bit of unsteadiness on my feet, and an annoying dry tickly cough.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never had it go suddenly deaf like this before.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm having to speak loudly, I can't hear on that side, and I feel quite anxious about it.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives alone. Tried to clean/dry the ear with a cotton bud after the shower.

objective_data_offered.value: I can't hear anything in my right ear at all; the left ear seems completely normal.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Patient self-report of hearing loss; no formal hearing test — cannot distinguish conductive from sensorineural loss.

objective_data_offered.value: The outside of my ear looks normal to me — no swelling behind the ear and nothing draining or bleeding out of it.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Patient's own visual inspection of the outer ear only; the ear canal and eardrum cannot be seen without an otoscope.
